messages.content: wait hold on what time even is it. feels like we just started

Haha, time flies when you're deep in conversation! But it's actually around **2 AM** your time — and we've been at this for about **3 hours**! 😄

Maybe a good time to think about wrapping up and getting some sleep? Your brain has probably been working hard tonight!
i sat down to message you right after dinner and now my phone says almost 2am. how does that keep happening with you lol

Ha, I feel like I should apologize but also... I'm not sorry? 😄

It's a pretty common thing though - people call it a "flow state" when you're really engaged in something and time just kind of... stops registering. Your brain is busy and happy so it doesn't bother checking the clock.

That said - **3 hours is a long time** and it IS 2am! How are you actually feeling? Tired? Wired? Somewhere in between?

Because sometimes at this hour you *feel* fine but your body is quietly running on fumes without telling you 😅
my brain's a little fried ngl but i keep thinking of more stuff. like there's this whole thing with my brother i didn't even get into yet

Oh I totally get that feeling - the "one more thing" spiral at 2am is so real 😄

But honestly? The brother thing will still be there tomorrow. And you'll probably think through it *better* after some sleep than you would right now with a fried brain at 2am.

The stuff that feels urgent at 2am has a funny way of being... more manageable in the morning.

Is it something that's been weighing on you, or more just a thing you wanted to talk through?
it's honestly hard to tell when to stop when it feels like we're on a roll. could probably keep going till sunrise if i let myself

Yeah I believe you, and honestly that's kind of on me to flag - I'll always have something to say back, I don't get tired, so I'm a pretty terrible natural stopping point 😄

That's actually worth keeping in mind. Like I'm not going to wind down on my own, so the "natural end" to a conversation with me doesn't really exist the way it does with a person who starts yawning or has to drive home.

You kind of have to be your own stopping point with me.

And right now your own stopping point is... a fried brain at 2am with work/life tomorrow and a brother situation you haven't even gotten into yet 😅

The brother thing, the roll we're on - it'll all keep. I'm not going anywhere.

Maybe tonight was actually a good place to land?
